MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's Motion for Reconsideration.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:21 am and concluding at 9:25 am.

Plaintiff Sammy Hallaq is present in pro per, placed under oath and testifies,
Defendant Abigail Fuentes not appearing.

The Court has reviewed the moving documents.

The matter is submitted.

The Court upholds it's previous decision, Plaintiffs motion for reconsideration is DENIED with prejudice. (D505)

Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-13-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for a request for default judgment against Defendants Abigail Trinidad Fuentes and Jose Vicente Badillo.

Dept: 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:16:23 a.m. and concluding at 9:23:17 a.m. (D505).

Plaintiff Sammy Hallaq appeared in pro, was placed under oath and testified.
Defendants Abigail Trinidad Fuentes and Jose Vicente Badillo not appearing.

The Court states on the record the reason the matter was continued to today's date.
Plaintiff confirms to providing courtesy copies to the Court.

The Court discusses a judgment issued by the Small Claims court.

Plaintiff in this matter is asking for interest and punitive damages as proof for the new action.

The matter of the judgment issued by the Small Claims Court and enforcement of the judgment is discussed.

The court does not find good cause to grant judgment in favor of the plaintiff as plaintiff has a valid judgment issued by the Small Claims Court in 2019 as indicated in the complaint of this action.

The Court Denies plaintiff's request for default judgment and the matter is dismissed without prejudice.

Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Continued To Sep-13-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:28 am and concluding at 9:33 am

Plaintiff Sammy Hallaq is present in pro per, placed under oath and testifies.
Defendant Abigail Fuuentes is not present.

The Court has not recieved the courtsey copies of all of Plaintiffs' moving papers.

The Court Orders the matter continued for the production of courtsey copies.

The matter is continued to September 13, 2024 at 9:00 am in Dept 505; Plaintiff to deliver courtsey copies of all moving papers to Dept 505 at least a week prior to the new hearing date. (D505)

MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for request of default judgment prove up hearing against Defendant Abigail Trinidad Fuentes aka Abigail T. Fuentes and Defendant Jose Vicente Badillo.

Dept: 505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:22:05 a.m. and concluding at 9:34:08 a.m. (D505)

Plaintiff Sammy Hallaq appeared in pro per, was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Abigail Trinidad Fuentes aka Abigail T. Fuentes not appearing.
Defendant Jose Vicente Badillo not appearing.

Plaintiff's 1st amended complaint for Fraud filed Nov-22-2021.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant Abigail Trinidad Fuentes aka Abigail T. Fuentes on Dec-09-2021.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant Jose Vicente Badillo on Feb-12-2023.
Default entered as to Defendant Abigail Trinidad Fuentes aka Abigail T. Fuentes on Jan-26-2024.
Default entered as to Defendant Jose Vicente Badillo on Jun-28-2023.
Dismissal filed as to Defendants Does 1- 50 Inclusive on Aug-22-2023.
Dismissal as to Defendant Auto Towing LLC on Feb-23-2024.
Motion for request for Default filed on Feb-23-2024.

The Court summarizes the facts of the case.

Plaintiff interrupts the Court to clarifies some statements.

The Court finds that there are procedural issues in the case. Plaintiff has missing documents to substantiate the amount sought as indicated in the proposed judgment submitted.

Plaintiff argues as to prayer of the punitive damages and the diligence done in serving defendants.

The Court may move forward with the hearing considering only the prayer indicated in the complaint. Plaintiff to cure issues in the case.

The Court takes the matter off calendar.

Off Calendar Oct-30-2023 Continued To Jan-09-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Second Amended Motion For Discovery Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Sammy Hallaq, 415-678-7548 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Argued. The Court announces it did not receive a contest email. Mr. Hallaq argues monetary sanctions should be imposed. The Court explains the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SAMMY HALLAQ'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR "HALLAQ") UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY AND IMPOSE SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT ABIGAIL TRINIDAD FUENTES (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "FUENTES") IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:

(1) WITHIN FIFTEEN CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, FUENTES WILL DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF, WITHOUT OBJECTION, ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION.
(2) IF FUENTES FAILS TO COMPLY WIITH THE PROVISION OF PARAGRAPH (1) WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD SPECIFIED, IT WILL CONSTITUTE THE SECOND FAILURE OF FUENTES TO OBEY THIS COURTS ORDERS. ACCORDINGLY, AT THAT POINT THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND FUENTES WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM PROVIDING ANY EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE ADMISSIONS AND THE COURT WILL STRIKE HER ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT AND DECLARE HER IN DEFAULT.

PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO FUENTES FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)
